$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-OVERALIGNED-STACK-LOCAL): an `alignas(32) int loc;` local exceeds the 16-byte stack-alignment bound this static frame layout can guarantee → the callconv frame-layout pass fails loud with L_OverAlignedStackLocal, NOT a silent under-align. The diagnostic is raised at the LIR calling-convention tier (computeFrameLayout), which carries NO source span/buffer, so the CLI renders it CODE-ONLY (`error[L_OverAlignedStackLocal]`) with no `:line:col` — the honest output of a span-less tier. Hence `positioned:false`: the in-process runner matches the CODE (position-independent) and the CLI/integrated harness greps for `error[L_OverAlignedStackLocal]`. The load-bearing pin is the CODE + a rejected compile (EXACT diagnostic-set equality). Red-on-disable: drop the computeFrameLayout `maxLocalAlign > stackAlignment` gate and this compiles (silently under-aligning) instead of erroring, so the runner no longer finds L_OverAlignedStackLocal.
